description_long: This is a classical acoustic guitar with a natural wood finish, featuring a light-colored spruce top and darker wooden sides and back. The body is hourglass-shaped with a circular sound hole at the center, surrounded by a decorative rosette. The neck is made of a lighter wood and leads up to a slotted headstock with three tuning pegs on each side, typical of classical guitars. The back and sides display a rich, reddish-brown wood grain, likely mahogany or rosewood. The bridge and fretboard are darker, possibly made from rosewood or ebony, and the guitar is strung with six nylon strings. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional, elegant design suitable for both performance and display in urban environments.
description: A classical acoustic guitar with a light spruce top, dark wooden back and sides, and a slotted headstock with six tuning pegs. It features a decorative rosette and six nylon strings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the light spruce top, circular sound hole with rosette, dark bridge, and full fretboard.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the guitar's depth, curved body, and the transition from the light top to the dark sides.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rich, dark wood grain of the back and the full length of the neck and headstock.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the guitar's thickness and the smooth curve of the body.
attribute_headstock_design: Slotted headstock with three tuning pegs per side, no visible logo, classical guitar style
